focus: Driver confidence collapse, draw bias effectiveness, forgiving driver debate, ego vs practicality

PAIN.quote: I have lost all ability to hit the driver or any woods either off a tee or off the deck. 90% of them are 50 yard tops or at best a low hook. This has occured more or less within the last 3-4 months
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Absolutely lost all confidence with driver and woods
PAIN.context: Lost all driver ability in 3-4 months

PAIN.quote: I used to usually get a slice of varying severity which while frustrating, never hurts or causes embarrassment like top shot after top shot. Which then completely ruins the remainder of the round
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Absolutely lost all confidence with driver and woods

PAIN.quote: I felt recently that I was saying to the guys and to myself I'll try the driver/3 wood the next day, just a little more practice on the range and will try to start back with it next week, but next week never comes
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Absolutely lost all confidence with driver and woods

PAIN.quote: To call it a disaster would be an understatement. I attempted to use the 3 wood off the tee and I scratched 7 of the first 9 holes. My whole game was effected and I lost all heart and couldn't even swing my irons
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Absolutely lost all confidence with driver and woods

PAIN.quote: I also play with a society and the last couple of outings were very awkward and uncomfortable. People constantly make comments which they think are helpful but in fact have the opposite effect
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Absolutely lost all confidence with driver and woods

PAIN.quote: My frustration was added to recently when I was asked to play in a scramble which I understood to be better ball all the way through but turned out that 3 of everyone's drives had to used. The guys I was playing with tried to hide their frustrations with my 200 yard tee shot but I could sense it
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Absolutely lost all confidence with driver and woods

PAIN.quote: I've had numerous lessons over the years and nothing fixed my slice. That'll say more about me as a student than the teachers though!
PAIN.context: Multiple lessons failed to fix slice

PAIN.quote: I suffer with a pretty pants club release with my driver and seem to be leaving the face too open, do these drivers actually help with this?
PAIN.context: Can't release the club face properly

HOPE.quote: I have the Mizuno ST-X 220. Best purchase I've made in a long time. I rarely lose a drive right these days, certainly no power fades in evidence. My driving has been the weak link in my game for a long time - it's now a strength
HOPE.context: Draw bias turned weakness to strength

HOPE.quote: They definitely work but they're not miracle workers. It won't turn a slice into a draw but it will turn one into a fade that's in play. Source: have used a G410 SFT for the last three years
HOPE.context: 3 years with SFT - slice now in play

HOPE.quote: I have the SFT, and love it. I can still slice it, and have yet to hit a proper draw after a year of ownership. However, I am rarely 3 off the tee or in real trouble left or right with it. It's certainly straightened my driving out
HOPE.context: SFT eliminated re-tees and big trouble

HOPE.quote: I picked up a TaylorMade Stealth HD Driver with a 12 degree head. For me it's been a bit of a game changer, I have definitely lost a bit of distance however it's keeping me on the cut stuff far more regularly which makes golf more fun
HOPE.context: Stealth HD = game changer despite distance loss

HOPE.quote: If playing with nothing more than a 3 iron works for you then stick with it - for the moment I'd take the woods out of the bag and leave them at home. Golf is as much about learning to play with your idiosyncrasies as it is about ironing them out
HOPE.source: TheSandTrap - Absolutely lost all confidence with driver and woods

HOPE.quote: I tried Fragger's G425 SFT and I couldn't even get it to fade...managed a few straight ones but mostly big draws
HOPE.context: SFT so effective fades become draws

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: I bet with your irons you are making a more controlled swing, but with the woods you are trying for distance. Build up your swing by making solid contact in the middle of the club face
ROOT_CAUSE.source: TheSandTrap - Absolutely lost all confidence with driver and woods

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: I played with a slice because of a long career of baseball and golf as an afterthought. When I took up golf seriously and started getting a real golf swing, then my 3 wood and driver COMPLETELY failed
ROOT_CAUSE.source: TheSandTrap - Absolutely lost all confidence with driver and woods

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: In clubs it is often difficult for many players to avoid succumbing at least a little bit to ego and hence avoid being seen playing a maximum forgiving or anti-slice driver head
ROOT_CAUSE.source: GolfWRX - Why don't we use more forgiving drivers?

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: I'm sure that I'm not the only one, but I've played with many others that like distance more than fairways. There are many that choose that one 300 yd drive per round over splitting fairway
ROOT_CAUSE.source: GolfWRX - Why don't we use more forgiving drivers?

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: As a result of this I intentionally left the driver and woods at home and hit my 3 iron off the tee and the fairway to a reasonable degree of success
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: TheSandTrap - Absolutely lost all confidence with driver and woods

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: I play a shorter shaft in my driver to optimize face contact, which makes my driver more forgiving
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: GolfWRX - Why don't we use more forgiving drivers?

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: I found it impossible to slice but wanted to fix my swing rather than buy a draw biased club and I have went back and forth with just giving in and buying one
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.context: Torn between fixing swing vs buying solution

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: I played behind a guy last week that was just awful with his driver. His problem was he thought he could swing like John Daly and get John Daly's results. Nothing wrong with a 3/4 swing that's in play
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: TheSandTrap - Absolutely lost all confidence with driver and woods

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: The Ping G series SFT are about as draw bias as you can get....one reviewer on YouTube said the furthest right he could hit a G430 SFT was 25 yards left
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.context: G430 SFT - impossible to miss right

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: GolfWRX - Why don't we use more forgiving drivers?
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.context: PING Max 10K user proud of choice

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: Checking in with the Callaway Rogue ST Max. It's the most forgiving driver I've ever played
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: GolfWRX - Why don't we use more forgiving drivers?
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.context: Rogue ST Max = most forgiving ever

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: But a number of these newer more forgiving heads, like the Cally Max D, are much more than an anti-slice driver. Worth exploring
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: GolfWRX - Why don't we use more forgiving drivers?

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: It depends on how you define forgiveness. The only real improvement in drivers over the past few years has been in 'forgiveness' in the sense that you can hit them all over the face without losing ball speed
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: GolfWRX - Why don't we use more forgiving drivers?

VILLAIN.quote: The easiest thing to do would be to give up but that's just not me. I love this game after only 2+ years of playing but really seem to have lost my way
VILLAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Absolutely lost all confidence with driver and woods
VILLAIN.context: 2 year golfer nearly ready to quit

VILLAIN.quote: I felt like crap for the round
VILLAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Absolutely lost all confidence with driver and woods

VILLAIN.quote: I just can't feel any rhythm, timing or balance like I do with the irons. But strangely, I can easily feel a nice swing during my practice swings with the driver/woods but put me over a ball and well........disaster
VILLAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Absolutely lost all confidence with driver and woods
VILLAIN.context: Practice swing vs real swing disconnect

VILLAIN.quote: They won't fix a bad swing, they may help to limit the impact of said bad swing. You could pull the trigger on it or spend less to take a few lessons and fix the swing flaw

sources_covered: TheSandTrap - Absolutely lost all confidence with driver and woods
GolfWRX - Why don't we use more forgiving drivers?
high_value_themes_identified: Lost all driver ability in 3-4 months
Multiple lessons failed to fix slice
Draw bias turned weakness to strength
SFT eliminated re-tees and big trouble
Practice swing vs real swing disconnect
